reasoning: The baseline value of one plus the 30-day average of 'Battles' over the past 360 days is 147.583. For this question to resolve positively, ACLED would need to record more than 1,475 battles (10 × 147.583) in the 30-day periods preceding February 22, 2026, and March 17, 2026. Given that the total number of battles for the entire preceding year was roughly 1,759 (an average of ~147 per month), achieving ~1,476 battles in a single month would require a 10x spike representing almost an entire year's worth of typical conflict events compressed into 30 days. While violence has escalated recently with the fall of Goma in late January 2025, a sudden ten-fold increase in the coding of distinct tactical battle events is virtually unprecedented in ACLED's DRC monitoring without a massive methodology change — which a review of ACLED's latest reports shows hasn't occurred. It would require approximately 50 distinct battles per day, every day, for a month. This crosses the threshold of physical and reporting implausibility for the DRC conflict context. Thus, I assign the minimum allowable probability (0.02) for the first resolution date, and slightly increase it to 0.03 for the second to reflect increased uncertainty over the longer time horizon.
